description: Configure GPredict for the NIST time laboratory by adding the CelesTrak GNSS TLE data source (https://celestrak.org/NORAD/elements/gnss.txt) and updating the satellite database. Create a ground station named 'NIST_Boulder' at 39.9936 N, 105.2618 W, and 1655 meters altitude, then set it as the default ground station. Create a new module named 'GNSS_Timing' containing the following satellites: NAVSTAR 82 (NORAD 55268), NAVSTAR 81 (NORAD 48859), GALILEO 22 (NORAD 43058), and GALILEO 26 (NORAD 43565). Configure the application to display time in UTC. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'. GPredict is already open.

natural_language.prompt: I need you to set up GPredict for our GNSS timing lab at NIST in Boulder. First, you need to add the GNSS TLE feed to GPredict's network update sources (the URL is https://celestrak.org/NORAD/elements/gnss.txt) and run the network update so we actually have the navigation satellites in the database. Next, create our ground station: call it NIST_Boulder, located at 39.9936 North, 105.2618 West, with an altitude of 1655 meters, and set it as the default ground station. Then, create a tracking module named GNSS_Timing and add these four satellites to it: NAVSTAR 82 (NORAD 55268), NAVSTAR 81 (NORAD 48859), GALILEO 22 (NORAD 43058), and GALILEO 26 (NORAD 43565). Finally, make sure GPredict is set to display UTC time, not local time.
